overview: The Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee approved two rezoning petitions unanimously and voted to forward a modified fee waiver resolution to the county board that will only waive reconnect fees for septic systems (not other zoning or pouts fees) for properties impacted by the April 17, 2026 tornado. The committee also received educational presentations from the Solid Waste, Parks Recreation and Forestry, and Conservation Planning and Zoning departments.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Public hearing: Tim Veland rezoning petition (Green Valley)
Public hearing: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (McMillan)
Consideration of fee waiver for properties impacted by April 17, 2026 severe weather
Educational presentation: Introduction to committee roles and responsibilities
Educational presentation: Solid Waste Department overview
Educational presentation: Parks, Recreation, and Forestry Department overview
Educational presentation: Conservation Planning and Zoning Department overview

discussions.body: Supervisor Sefeld made a motion to approve the minutes, seconded by Supervisor Drebeck. The motion carried unanimously with no discussion.

Garrett Pogle presented the staff report for rezoning from Urban Residential and General Agriculture to Rural Residential in the Town of Green Valley. The Town Board recommended approval on March 10, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Drebeck moved to approve based on findings of fact, conclusions of law, and town approval. Supervisor Kurt seconded. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (McMillan)
discussions.body: Garrett Pogle presented the petition to rezone from Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture in the Town of McMillan. Supervisor Leur asked about the conservancy nature of the property; staff explained it currently has a home and horse riding stable, and rezoning would make these conforming uses. The Town Board recommended approval on April 20, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Sefeld moved to approve, Supervisor Drew seconded. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Fee waiver for April 17, 2026 severe weather impacted properties
discussions.body: Director Lori Mskimmons presented a proposal to waive county zoning and pouts fees for properties damaged by the April 17th tornado in the towns of Ringle, Easton, and villages of Kronenwetter and Weston. Supervisor Drew questioned where to draw the line for future events. Supervisor Drebeck expressed concerns about fairness to property owners impacted by disasters not declared emergencies. After discussion, Supervisor Sefeld moved to forward the resolution to the county board. Supervisor Leur seconded. Supervisor Drebeck then moved to amend the resolution to only waive reconnect fees for pouts systems, not other zoning or pouts fees. Supervisor Kurt seconded the amendment. The amendment carried. The amended motion to forward to county board passed, though not unanimously.

Director Dave Hagen Busher presented on the department's operations, explaining they accept about 1,000 tons of waste daily with 11 staff members. He highlighted the renewable natural gas partnership with Vidi Energy, significant capital expenses for landfill expansion and closure, and upcoming requirements by 2032 for leachate treatment due to PFAS concerns. Marilyn Bend commended the department for keeping independent landfills competitive.

discussions.item: Parks, Recreation, and Forestry Department overview
discussions.body: Tom Lovelin, County Forest Administrator, presented on behalf of Director Jamie who had an emergency. He noted the department serves both Marathon County and City of Wausau with 44 full-time and 120 seasonal staff. He highlighted Nine Mile County Forest generates $3.25 million for the local economy from out-of-town visitors. Supervisor Sefeld announced Tom is retiring June 1st after nearly 30 years with the county, with Joe Tucker selected as his replacement.

actionItems: Rezoning petition for James Offer and David Tract (Green Valley) approved and forwarded to county board
Rezoning petition for Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz (McMillan) approved and forwarded to county board
Modified fee waiver resolution (reconnect fees only) for April 17, 2026 tornado-impacted properties forwarded to county board
Staff to track costs and requests related to fee waivers for potential future policy development
Conservation services educational presentation scheduled for next meeting with Kirsty Heiden
title: Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee Meeting
